title: Cannot set property 'outlineProperties' of null (v3.8.2)
author: tpkn
body:
This issue is closed https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/issues/655, but that bug is still exists:

    TypeError: Cannot set property 'outlineProperties' of null
        at WorkSheetXform.parseClose (E:\test\parser\node_modules\exceljs\lib\xlsx\xform\sheet\worksheet-xform.js:388:40)
        at saxStream.sax.on.node (E:\test\parser\node_modules\exceljs\lib\xlsx\xform\base-xform.js:91:21)
        at SaxesParser.closeTag (E:\test\parser\node_modules\saxes\saxes.js:2012:65)
        at SaxesParser.sCloseTag (E:\test\parser\node_modules\saxes\saxes.js:1601:18)
        at SaxesParser.write (E:\test\parser\node_modules\saxes\saxes.js:483:36)
        at SAXStream._write (E:\test\parser\node_modules\exceljs\lib\utils\sax-stream.js:11:14)
        at doWrite (_stream_writable.js:415:12)
        at writeOrBuffer (_stream_writable.js:399:5)
        at SAXStream.Writable.write (_stream_writable.js:299:11)
        at resolve (E:\test\parser\node_modules\exceljs\lib\utils\stream-buf.js:205:14)
        at new Promise (<anonymous>)
        at write (E:\test\parser\node_modules\exceljs\lib\utils\stream-buf.js:204:14)
        at Array.map (<anonymous>)
        at StreamBuf._pipe (E:\test\parser\node_modules\exceljs\lib\utils\stream-buf.js:210:34)
        at StreamBuf._flush (E:\test\parser\node_modules\exceljs\lib\utils\stream-buf.js:275:14)
        at writeComplete (E:\test\parser\node_modules\exceljs\lib\utils\stream-buf.js:288:14)


v3.8.2

comments.author: Alanscut
Alanscut
comments.body:
@tpkn Please tell me the version of excel software you are using, because I found that the structure of the xml file in **text.xlsx** is very different from my newly created file, I can’t know if it needs to be compatible.

## For example
* The ``target``  attribute of **text.xlsx** is an absolute path and the other is a relative path.
```xml.
//  workbook.xml
<Relationship Type="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships/worksheet" Target="/xl/worksheets/sheet1.xml" Id="rId6" />

<Relationship Id="rId1" Type="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships/worksheet" Target="worksheets/sheet1.xml" />
```
* The **sheet1.xml** in the newly created file should have the ```sheetFormatPr``` tag and default attributes, but the **test.xlsx** file does not have.
```xml
// sheet1.xml
 <sheetFormatPr defaultRowHeight="13.5" x14ac:dyDescent="0.15" />
````

comments.author: tpkn
comments.body:
@Alanscut the whole engine that creates that xlsx files is a black box. 

Is it possible to inject `sheetFormatPr` node with manually setted attributes?

comments.author: Alanscut
tpkn
comments.body: @Alanscut example crashes with the same error.

comments.author: Alanscut
